panels.title: Node
panels.description: **Node**`n`nShows the currently selected exporter target and provides a quick link context for the selected MySQL node scope.

panels.title: MySQL Uptime
panels.description: **MySQL Uptime**`n`nShows how long the selected MySQL service has been running since its last restart. Use it to confirm stability and detect recent failovers or restarts.

panels.title: Version
panels.description: **Version**`n`nDisplays the MySQL server version reported for the current selection. Use it to verify version consistency across instances.

panels.title: Current QPS
panels.description: **Current QPS**`n`nShows the current queries-per-second rate based on MySQL status counters. Use it as a primary indicator of workload intensity.

links.title: Tuning the InnoDB Buffer Pool Size

panels.title: InnoDB Buffer Pool Size
panels.description: **InnoDB Buffer Pool Size**`n`nShows the configured InnoDB buffer pool size for the selected MySQL service. Use it to understand the memory allocated for caching InnoDB data and indexes.

links.title: Tuning the InnoDB Buffer Pool Size

targets.expr: 100 * (avg by (service_name)(mysql_global_variables_innodb_buffer_pool_size{service_name=~"$service_name"})) / on(service_name) min by (service_name) (node_memory_MemTotal_bytes * on(node_name) group_left(service_name) min by (node_name,service_name) (mysql_up{service_name=~"$service_name"}))

panels.title: Buffer Pool Size of Total RAM
panels.description: **Buffer Pool Size of Total RAM**`n`nShows the share of total host memory allocated to the InnoDB buffer pool. Use it to assess whether memory allocation is aligned with the server role.
**Service Summary**`n`nCommunity-edition compatible summary of the current dashboard selection, including service, instance, node IP, port, exporter job, and detected MySQL version.
panels.title: Service Summary
MySQL Connections
panels.description: **MySQL Connections**`n`nCompares active connections, peak used connections, and configured maximum connections. Use it to detect connection pressure before the instance reaches saturation.

panels.title: MySQL Aborted Connections
panels.description: **MySQL Aborted Connections**`n`nShows failed or interrupted client connection activity. Rising values can indicate authentication problems, unstable clients, or network-related disconnects.

panels.title: MySQL Client Thread Activity
panels.description: **MySQL Client Thread Activity**`n`nShows connected, running, and average running threads for the selected service. Use it to understand concurrency and active session pressure.

panels.title: MySQL Thread Cache
panels.description: **MySQL Thread Cache**`n`nShows thread cache size, cached threads, and thread creation rate. Use it to determine whether MySQL is reusing threads efficiently.

panels.title: MySQL Temporary Objects
panels.description: **MySQL Temporary Objects**`n`nShows rates for temporary tables, temporary disk tables, and temporary files. Rising disk-based temporary objects can indicate sort pressure or inefficient query execution.

panels.title: MySQL Slow Queries
panels.description: **MySQL Slow Queries**`n`nShows the rate of queries recorded as slow by MySQL. Use it to identify periods where query latency is exceeding expected thresholds.

panels.title: MySQL Select Types
panels.description: **MySQL Select Types**`n`nShows the rate of different select execution patterns such as full joins and range scans. Use it to identify inefficient access paths and missing indexes.

panels.title: MySQL Sorts
panels.description: **MySQL Sorts**`n`nShows sort activity across the selected service. Use it to identify workloads that are spending significant effort sorting result sets.

panels.title: MySQL Table Locks
panels.description: **MySQL Table Locks**`n`nShows immediate and waited table locks. Rising waited locks can indicate lock contention affecting query throughput.

panels.title: MySQL Questions
panels.description: **MySQL Questions**`n`nShows the rate of statements sent by clients to the server. Use it alongside QPS to understand client request volume and workload shape.

panels.title: MySQL Network Traffic
panels.description: **MySQL Network Traffic**`n`nShows inbound and outbound network throughput for MySQL. Use it to spot shifts in client traffic patterns or unusually chatty workloads.

panels.title: MySQL Network Usage Hourly
panels.description: **MySQL Network Usage Hourly**`n`nShows hourly network volume sent and received by MySQL. Use it for higher-level trend comparison across recent periods.

panels.title: MySQL Internal Memory Overview
panels.description: **MySQL Internal Memory Overview**`n`nShows major MySQL internal memory structures such as buffer pools, caches, and log buffers. Use it to understand how memory is allocated inside the database engine.

panels.title: Top Command Counters
panels.description: **Top Command Counters**`n`nShows the most active MySQL command counters for the selected service. Use it to identify the dominant statement types driving workload.

panels.title: Top Command Counters Hourly
panels.description: **Top Command Counters Hourly**`n`nShows the leading command counters aggregated hourly. Use it to compare statement mix over a longer trend window.

panels.title: MySQL Handlers
panels.description: **MySQL Handlers**`n`nShows internal MySQL handler activity such as index reads and row navigation operations. Use it to infer access patterns below the SQL layer.

panels.title: MySQL Transaction Handlers
panels.description: **MySQL Transaction Handlers**`n`nShows commit, rollback, savepoint, and prepare activity. Use it to understand transaction volume and detect rollback-heavy behavior.

panels.title: Process States
panels.description: **Process States**`n`nShows MySQL thread and session states for the selected service. Use it to spot blocking, idle buildup, or unusual time spent in specific execution states.

panels.title: MySQL Query Cache Memory
panels.description: **MySQL Query Cache Memory**`n`nShows query cache memory components and usage. Use it mainly for legacy environments where query cache is still enabled.

panels.title: MySQL Query Cache Activity
panels.description: **MySQL Query Cache Activity**`n`nShows query cache hits, inserts, prunes, and related activity. Use it to judge whether query cache behavior is helping or adding contention.

panels.title: MySQL File Openings
panels.description: **MySQL File Openings**`n`nShows the rate at which MySQL opens files. Sustained increases can indicate cache inefficiency, object churn, or file descriptor pressure.

panels.title: MySQL Open Files
panels.description: **MySQL Open Files**`n`nShows current open file usage against configured limits. Use it to detect whether the service is approaching file descriptor constraints.

panels.title: MySQL Table Open Cache Status
panels.description: **MySQL Table Open Cache Status**`n`nShows table cache openings, hits, misses, and overflow-related misses. Use it to evaluate whether table cache settings are sized appropriately.

panels.title: MySQL Open Tables
panels.description: **MySQL Open Tables**`n`nShows current open tables compared with configured table open cache. Use it to understand table cache occupancy and pressure.

panels.title: MySQL Table Definition Cache
panels.description: **MySQL Table Definition Cache**`n`nShows open and opened table definition activity versus configured cache size. Use it to assess table definition cache efficiency.

panels.title: Maintained By
panels.description: **Maintained By**`n`nShows dashboard ownership and maintenance information for operational support and change accountability.
